title: [LV] Allow loops with multiple early exits in legality checks.
author: fhahn
description:
This patch removes the single uncountable exit constraint, allowing loops with multiple early exits, if the exits form a dominance chain and all other constraints hold for all uncountable early exits.

While legality now accepts such loops, vectorization is not yet supported. VPlan support will be added in a follow up: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174864

tests.test_body:
declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @uncountable_exit_in_conditional_block(ptr %mask) {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %mask, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp ne i8 %ld1, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %loop.search, label %loop.inc

loop.search:                                      ; preds = %loop
  %arrayidx2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx2, align 1
  %arrayidx3 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld3 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx3, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld2, %ld3
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop.search, %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop.search
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %loop.search ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @uncountable_exits_on_parallel_branches() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %latch ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp sgt i8 %ld1, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %left, label %right

left:                                             ; preds = %header
  %arrayidx.left = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld.left = load i8, ptr %arrayidx.left, align 1
  %cmp.left = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld.left
  br i1 %cmp.left, label %loop.end, label %latch

right:                                            ; preds = %header
  %cmp.right = icmp ult i8 %ld1, 34
  br i1 %cmp.right, label %loop.end, label %latch

latch:                                            ; preds = %right, %left
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %latch, %right, %left
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %left ], [ 100, %right ], [ 43, %latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @multiple_uncountable_exits() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %search1

search1:                                          ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp1, label %loop.end, label %search2

search2:                                          ; preds = %search1
  %cmp2 = icmp ult i8 %ld1, 34
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop.end, label %loop.inc

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %search2
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %search1, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %search2, %search1
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %search1 ], [ 100, %search2 ], [ 43, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @uncountable_exit_on_last_block() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %search, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %search ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %search, label %loop.end

search:                                           ; preds = %loop
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %search, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ 64, %loop ], [ %index, %search ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

define void @loop_contains_store_uncounted_exit_is_not_guaranteed_to_execute(ptr noalias dereferenceable(40) %array, ptr readonly align 2 dereferenceable(40) %pred) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.inc ]
  %st.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %array, i64 %iv
  %data = load i16, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %inc = add nsw i16 %data, 1
  store i16 %inc, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %rem = urem i64 %iv, 5
  %skip.ee.cmp = icmp eq i64 %rem, 0
  br i1 %skip.ee.cmp, label %for.inc, label %ee.block

ee.block:                                         ; preds = %for.body
  %ee.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %pred, i64 %iv
  %ee.val = load i16, ptr %ee.addr, align 2
  %ee.cond = icmp sgt i16 %ee.val, 500
  br i1 %ee.cond, label %exit, label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %ee.block, %for.body
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %counted.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 20
  br i1 %counted.cond, label %exit, label %for.body

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.inc, %ee.block
  ret void
}

define void @crash_conditional_load_for_uncountable_exit_argptr(ptr noalias dereferenceable(40) %store.area, ptr dereferenceable(4) %load.area, i1 %skip.cond) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.inc ]
  %ee.addr = getelementptr i8, ptr %load.area, i64 %iv
  br i1 %skip.cond, label %ee.block, label %invalid.block

ee.block:                                         ; preds = %for.body
  %ee.val = load i8, ptr %ee.addr, align 1
  store i16 0, ptr %store.area, align 2
  %ee.cmp = icmp eq i8 %ee.val, 0
  br i1 %ee.cmp, label %for.inc, label %invalid.block

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %ee.block
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %counted.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 10
  br i1 %counted.cond, label %invalid.block, label %for.body

invalid.block:                                    ; preds = %for.inc, %ee.block, %for.body
  unreachable
}

declare void @init(ptr)

define i64 @two_early_exits_same_exit_with_constant_live_outs() {
entry:
  %A = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %B = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init(ptr %A, i64 1024)
  call void @init(ptr %B, i64 1024)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %ld.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, 42
  br i1 %cmp1, label %exit, label %early.exit.0

early.exit.0:                                     ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %B, i64 %iv
  %ld.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i8 %ld.A, %ld.B
  br i1 %cmp2, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %early.exit.0
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %early.exit.0, %loop.header
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 100, %early.exit.0 ], [ 43, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

comments.author: joanahalili
comments.body:
Hello all, 

We have managed to reduce and bisect a clang crash caused by this revision.

`clang -O3 -c -o /dev/null test.ii`
where test.ii

```

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

@global = external constant <{ [248 x i8], [8 x i8] }>

define i8 @foo(i32 %arg) {
bb:
  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %arg, 1
  br i1 %icmp, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:                                              ; preds = %bb
  %load = load i8, ptr @global, align 1
  ret i8 %load

bb2:                                              ; preds = %bb
  %icmp3 = icmp sgt i32 %arg, 8205
  br i1 %icmp3, label %bb5, label %bb4

bb4:                                              ; preds = %bb2
  ret i8 0

bb5:                                              ; preds = %bb2
  %or = or i32 %arg, -8232
  %icmp6 = icmp ult i32 %or, -24
  %zext = zext i1 %icmp6 to i8
  ret i8 %zext
}

define i32 @eggs(i32 %arg) {
bb:
  br label %bb1

bb1:                                              ; preds = %bb5, %bb
  %phi = phi i32 [ %arg, %bb ], [ %add, %bb5 ]
  %icmp = icmp slt i32 %phi, 0
  br i1 %icmp, label %bb2, label %bb6

bb2:                                              ; preds = %bb1
  %call = call i16 @wibble(i32 %phi)
  %zext = zext i16 %call to i32
  %call3 = call i8 @foo(i32 %zext)
  %icmp4 = icmp eq i8 %call3, 0
  br i1 %icmp4, label %bb6, label %bb5

bb5:                                              ; preds = %bb2
  %add = add i32 %phi, 1
  br label %bb1

bb6:                                              ; preds = %bb2, %bb1
  ret i32 %phi
}

define i16 @wibble(i32 %arg) {
bb:
  %load = load volatile ptr, ptr null, align 8
  %sext = sext i32 %arg to i64
  %getelementptr = getelementptr i16, ptr %load, i64 %sext
  %load1 = load i16, ptr %getelementptr, align 2
  ret i16 %load1
}
```
The failure seems related to libc++ Hardening assertions. The following are the parts of the clang crash logs:
```
libcxx/include/__debug_utils/strict_weak_ordering_check.h:50: libc++ Hardening assertion !__comp(*(__first + __a), *(__first + __b)) failed: Your comparator is not a valid strict-weak ordering
PLEASE submit a bug report to http://go/clang-crash-bug and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: .../clang -O3 -c -o /dev/null test.ll
1.	Optimizer
2.	Running pass "function<eager-inv>...
3.	Running pass "loop-vectorize<no-interleave-forced-only;no-vectorize-forced-only;>" on function "eggs"
clang: error: clang frontend command failed with exit code 134 (use -v to see invocation)
```

Could you please have a look?

comments.author: alexfh
comments.body: @fhahn please take a look at the report above. Thanks!
